name: DatePicker

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: A type representing messages that are passed within the DatePicker.

unions.name: PresetInterval
unions.comment:
A type representing what the value in PresetSettings is measured in.

  - Ex. value = 1 and PresetInterval = Days, this is interpretted as 1 Days.
  - Ex. value = 4 and PresetI:nterval = Months, this is interpretted as 4 Months.

unions.name: PresetOption
unions.comment:
A type representing which presets to use.

  - _DefaultPresets_ = Use the default presets from defaultPresets only.
  - _CustomPresetsFromSettings_ = Use only your custom built presets from a list of PresetSetting only.
  - _CustomPresets_ = Use only your custom built Presets.
  - _CustomOnly_ = Use only your custom build presets build from PresetSetting and your custom presets.
  - _AllPresets_ = Use all presets (default, customFromSettings, custom).
  - _NoPresets_ = Turn off Presets.

unions.name: PresetRelativeToToday
unions.comment:
A type representing how the preset is relative to today.

  - BeforeToday will subtract any value from today and use that date.
  - AfterToday will add any value form today and use that date.
  - Today will use today.
  - Tomorrow will use tomorrow.
  - Yesterday will use yesterday.

aliases.name: Preset
aliases.comment:
A type that represents a preset date.

  - _name_ = Name of the preset. i.e. "Past Month"
  - _date_ = The date that is selected when selecting the preset.

aliases.name: PresetOptions
aliases.comment: A type representing your preset options for your date range picker.
aliases.type: { presetOption : DatePicker.PresetOption, presetSettings : List.List DatePicker.PresetSetting, presets : List.List DatePicker.Preset }

aliases.name: PresetSetting
aliases.comment:
A type used to generate preset dateranges.

  - _name_ = The name that you want to give the preset. i.e. "Past Month"
  - _interval_ = The interval in which you want to add/subtract the value from today.
  - _presetRelativeToToday_ = whether it is a range from [past - present] (ToToday) or [present - future] (FromToday)
  - _value_ = the number of your @interval that you are adding/subtracting.


## Example

    { name = "1 Month Ago"
    , interval = Months
    , presetRelativeToday = BeforeToday
    , value = 1
    }
aliases.type: { name : String.String, interval : DatePicker.PresetInterval, presetRelativeToToday : DatePicker.PresetRelativeToToday, value : Basics.Int }

aliases.name: Settings
aliases.comment: The settings that the DatePicker uses.
aliases.type: { placeholder : String.String, inputName : Maybe.Maybe String.String, inputId : Maybe.Maybe String.String, inputIcon : Maybe.Maybe (Html.Html DatePicker.Msg), inputAttributes : List.List (Html.Attribute DatePicker.Msg), presetOptions : DatePicker.PresetOptions, restrictedDateRange : DateRangePicker.Common.RestrictedDateRange, formatDate : Date.Date -> String.String, calendarDisplay : DateRangePicker.Common.CalendarDisplay }

The default initial state of the DatePicker. You must execute
the returned command in order to set the current date and for the
datepicker to behave correctly.

values.name: setInputId
values.comment: Sets the id for the datepicker.

values.name: setInputName
values.comment: Sets the name for the datepicker.

values.name: setOpen
values.comment: Sets the the open state of the DatePicker

values.name: setPlaceholder
values.comment: Sets the placeholder for the datepicker.

values.comment: Sets the preset options for the datepicker.

values.comment: Sets the settings for the daterange picker

values.name: view

name: DateRangePicker

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: A type representing messages that are passed within the DateRangePicker.

unions.name: PresetInterval
unions.comment:
A type representing what the value in PresetSettings is measured in.

  - Ex. value = 1 and PresetInterval = Days, this is interpretted as 1 Days.
  - Ex. value = 4 and PresetI:nterval = Months, this is interpretted as 4 Months.

unions.name: PresetOption
unions.comment:
A type representing which presets to use.

  - _DefaultPresets_ = Use the default presets from defaultPresets only.
  - _CustomPresetsFromSettings_ = Use only your custom built presets from a list of PresetSetting only.
  - _CustomPresets_ = Use only your custom built Presets.
  - _CustomOnly_ = Use only your custom build presets build from PresetSetting and your custom presets.
  - _AllPresets_ = Use all presets (default, customFromSettings, custom).
  - _NoPresets_ = Turn off Presets.

unions.name: PresetRelativeToToday
unions.comment:
A type representing how the preset is relative to today.

  - If using ToToday, the preset daterange would use today as the end date, and the date from your PresetSettings as the end date.
  - If using FromToday, the preset daterange would use today as the start date and the date from your PresetSettings as the end date.

aliases.name: Preset
aliases.comment:
A type that represents a preset daterange.

  - _name_ = Name of the preset. i.e. "Past Month"
  - _dateRange_ = The daterange that is selected when selecting the preset.

aliases.name: PresetOptions
aliases.comment: A type representing your preset options for your date range picker.
aliases.type: { presetOption : DateRangePicker.PresetOption, presetSettings : List.List DateRangePicker.PresetSetting, presets : List.List DateRangePicker.Preset }

aliases.name: PresetSetting
aliases.comment:
A type used to generate preset dateranges.

  - _name_ = The name that you want to give the preset. i.e. "Past Month"
  - _interval_ = The interval in which you want to add/subtract the value from today.
  - _presetRelativeToToday_ = whether it is a range from [past - present] (ToToday) or [present - future] (FromToday)
  - _value_ = the number of your @interval that you are adding/subtracting.


## Example

    { name = "Past Month"
    , interval = Months
    , presetRelativeToday = ToToday
    , value = 1
    }
aliases.type: { name : String.String, interval : DateRangePicker.PresetInterval, presetRelativeToToday : DateRangePicker.PresetRelativeToToday, value : Basics.Int }

aliases.name: Settings
aliases.comment: The settings that the DateRangePicker uses.
aliases.type: { placeholder : String.String, inputName : Maybe.Maybe String.String, inputId : Maybe.Maybe String.String, inputIcon : Maybe.Maybe (Html.Html DateRangePicker.Msg), inputAttributes : List.List (Html.Attribute DateRangePicker.Msg), presetOptions : DateRangePicker.PresetOptions, restrictedDateRange : DateRangePicker.Common.RestrictedDateRange, formatDateRange : DateRangePicker.Common.DateRange -> String.String, calendarDisplay : DateRangePicker.Common.CalendarDisplay }

name: DateRangePicker.Common

unions.name: CalendarDisplay
unions.comment:
A type representing how the calendar will be displayed

  - _FullCalendar_ = the datepicker displays all 12 months when open
  - _ThreeMonths_ = the datepicker displays 3 months at a time
  - _TwoMonths_ = the datepicker displays 2 months at a time
  - _OneMonth_ = the datepicker displays 1 month at a time

unions.name: RestrictedDateRange
unions.comment:
A type representing a restricted range for the datepicker. All dates not within the restricted date range will be disabled.

  - _Off_ = no restrictions, any date to any date can be chosen.
  - _ToPresent_ = from any date in the past up to today (including today)
  - _FromPresent_ = from today to any date in the future
  - _Past_ = from any date in the past up to yesterday (excluding today)
  - _Future_ = from tomorrow up to any date in the future
  - _Between_ date date = only between the two given dates [start - end] (inclusive)
  - _To_ date = from any date in the past up to the given date (inclusive)
  - _From_ date = from the given date up to any date in the future (inclusive)

aliases.name: DateRange
aliases.comment: A type representing a date range with a start date and end date.

values.name: calendarDisplayToClassStr
values.comment: A function that gets the class string for the CalendarDisplay

values.name: calendarDisplayToDisplayStr
values.comment: A function that gets the display string for the CalendarDisplay

values.name: daysInRange
values.comment: A function that returns the number of days as a whole number in the daterange

values.name: inRange
values.comment:
A function to check if a given date is within a
given dateRange.

values.name: mkDateRange
values.comment:
A function that creates a DateRange by taking in two dates (start and end).

This function assumes that start <= end

values.name: monthsInRange
values.comment: A function that returns the number of months as a whole number in the daterange

values.name: weeksInRange
values.comment: A function that returns the number of weeks as a whole number in the daterange

values.name: yearsInRange
values.comment: A function that returns the number of years as a whole number in the daterange

name: DateRangePicker.Date

values.name: dateEqualTo
values.comment: A function that checks if date a is equal to date b

values.name: dateGreaterThan
values.comment: A function that checks if date a is greater than to date b

values.name: dateGreaterThanOrEqualTo
values.comment: A function that checks if date a is greater than or equal to date b

values.name: dateLessThan
values.comment: A function that checks if date a is less than to date b

values.name: dateLessThanOrEqualTo
values.comment: A function that checks if date a is less than or equal to date b

values.name: dateTuple
values.comment: A function that converts a Date into a tuple. (year, month, day)

values.name: dayToInt
values.comment:
A function that takes a Weekday and returns it as an integer.

  - Sun - Sat
  - 1 - 7

values.name: daysInMonth
values.comment: A function that returns the number of days in the given month for the given year.

values.name: endOfMonth
values.comment: A function that takes a Date and returns the date representing the end of that month.

values.name: endOfQuarter
values.comment: A function that takes a Date and returns the date representing the last date of the quarter that the passed in date belongs to.

values.name: formatDate
values.comment:
A function to format a Date into a string.

  - Ex. Feb 15, 2018

values.name: formatDay
values.comment:
A function that takes a Weekday and formats it into the abbreviated string.

  - Ex. Mon -> "Mo"

values.name: formatMonth
values.comment:
A function that formats a Month into the full string.

  - Ex. Jan -> "January"

values.name: initDate
values.comment: A function that initializes a date.

values.name: monthAbbr
values.comment:
A function that formats a Month into the abbreviated string.

  - Ex. Jan -> "Jan"

values.name: monthToInt
values.comment:
A function that takes a Month and returns it as an Int.

  - Ex. Jan -> 1
  - Ex. Dec -> 12

values.name: startOfMonth
values.comment: A function that takes a Date and returns the date representing the first of that month.

values.name: startOfQuarter
values.comment: A function that takes a Date and returns the date representing the first date of the quarter that the passed in date belongs to.
